game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Bounce And Collect:** Players initiate a round by tapping the top of the screen to drop a single ball into a vertical, physics-based arena filled with pegs, gates, and multiplier zones. The ball bounces dynamically, interacting with the environment, aiming to pass through specific multiplier gates (e.g., x2, x5, +10 balls) to increase the total ball count or score before falling into collection bins at the bottom. Each level presents a unique static or dynamic layout of obstacles and multipliers, requiring strategic initial drop placement (e.g., by swiping left/right to position the drop point) to optimize ball multiplication and score accumulation. Progression involves completing a series of increasingly complex levels, each with a target score or a limited number of initial balls (e.g., 5 drops per level), unlocking new ball skins or starting bonuses (e.g., start with 2 balls) upon reaching specific cumulative score milestones. Difficulty scales through more intricate level designs featuring moving obstacles, smaller multiplier targets, and higher score requirements, with the primary goal being to achieve the highest possible score within the given constraints of each level.
game_info.description: Drop balls strategically into a physics-based arena filled with pegs, multipliers, and obstacles. Your goal is to maximize your score by positioning each ball drop to hit high-value multiplier gates (x2, x5, +10 balls) before landing in collection bins. Complete each level by reaching the target score with limited ball drops. Progress through increasingly complex levels with moving obstacles and unlock new ball skins.
game_info.controls:
LEFT ARROW: Move drop position left
RIGHT ARROW: Move drop position right
SPACE: Drop a ball from current position
ENTER: Start game (from START screen)
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart (from GAME_OVER screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic ball drop mechanics and physics simulation. Verifies that balls drop when SPACE is pressed, interact properly with pegs and walls using Matter.js physics, and eventually land in collection bins at the bottom. Also tests that the drop position can be controlled with arrow keys.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Drop 3 balls at different horizontal positions (left, center, right) by moving the drop position with arrow keys and pressing SPACE. Wait for each ball to complete its journey through the arena and land in a bin. Observe physics interactions with pegs and boundaries.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: All balls should drop smoothly, bounce realistically off pegs and walls, and eventually settle in bottom bins. The drop position indicator should move left/right with arrow keys. No balls should escape the arena or phase through obstacles. Physics should look natural and responsive.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing multiplier gates and score accumulation mechanics. Verifies that balls passing through multiplier zones correctly increase score or spawn additional balls. Tests the win condition by intentionally targeting high-value multipliers to reach the level target score quickly.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Strategically position drops to aim for visible multiplier gates (x2, x5, +10 balls zones). Drop multiple balls targeting the highest multipliers. Monitor score increases and additional ball spawns when multipliers are triggered. Continue until reaching the level target score to trigger win state.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Score should increase correctly when balls pass through multiplier zones. Ball count should increase when +balls multipliers are hit. Upon reaching the target score, game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state with appropriate victory message. All multiplier interactions should be visually clear and numerically accurate.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing level progression and difficulty scaling. Verifies that completing a level advances to the next with increased complexity, higher target scores, and more challenging obstacle layouts. Tests that game state properly resets between levels while maintaining cumulative progress.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Each subsequent level should have a distinct layout with more complex peg arrangements. Target scores should increase progressively. Ball count should reset but level number and cumulative achievements should persist. Moving obstacles should appear in later levels, making strategic drops more challenging.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing lose condition when running out of balls. Verifies that the game properly tracks remaining ball drops and transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE when all balls are used without reaching the target score. Tests restart functionality from game over state.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: When the last ball lands without reaching target score, game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state. Appropriate "Level Failed" message should display with final score and remaining target. Pressing R should restart and return to START screen, allowing replay.
TEST_4.description: Testing lose condition when running out of balls. Verifies that the game properly tracks remaining ball drops and transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE when all balls are used without reaching the target score. Tests restart functionality from game over state.
TEST_4.expected: When the last ball lands without reaching target score, game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state. Appropriate "Level Failed" message should display with final score and remaining target. Pressing R should restart and return to START screen, allowing replay.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing pause functionality and game state persistence. Verifies that pressing ESC freezes all physics and gameplay while maintaining game state, and unpausing resumes exactly where left off. Tests that player can pause mid-ball-drop without issues.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Pressing ESC should freeze all ball motion and physics simulation. Pause overlay should display clearly. Pressing ESC again should resume gameplay with balls continuing from their exact paused positions and velocities. Score, ball count, and level progress should remain unchanged. No physics glitches or position jumps should occur.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing collision detection and visual feedback for multipliers. Verifies that the game accurately detects when balls pass through multiplier zones and provides clear visual feedback. Tests that multiple balls can trigger multipliers simultaneously without conflicts.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Drop multiple balls in quick succession aimed at the same multiplier zone. Observe collision detection as each ball passes through. Look for visual effects (particles, flashes, color changes) that indicate successful multiplier activation. Verify score updates match expected multiplier values.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Each ball passing through a multiplier should trigger a clear visual effect (glow, particle burst, or color flash). Score should update immediately and accurately for each activation. Multiple simultaneous collisions should all register correctly without race conditions. Visual feedback should be distinct enough to track which multipliers were hit.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing edge cases and boundary conditions. Verifies that balls cannot move drop position out of bounds, that extreme drop positions near walls work correctly, and that the arena boundaries are solid. Tests performance with maximum balls on screen simultaneously.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Attempt to move drop position far left and right beyond arena bounds using arrow keys repeatedly. Drop balls from extreme edges to test wall collisions. Use multipliers to spawn many additional balls, creating maximum ball count on screen to test performance and physics stability.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Drop position should be clamped within valid arena bounds. Balls dropped near edges should bounce properly off walls without clipping through. With many balls on screen (10+), physics should remain stable and responsive at 60 FPS. No matter.js errors or visual artifacts should occur even with high ball counts.
